Hold thy purse close: a coin not spent today strengthens tomorrow's treasury.
If thy daily spending climbs above thy rolling average, slow thy hand before dusk.
A planned deed confirmed early keeps forecasted perils from becoming real ones.
When a peril nears, postpone luxuries and fortify thy essential coin first.
Small savings, repeated often, forge the strongest castle walls.
Name thy expenses clearly; what is measured is mastered.
If gold runs low, review unplanned outgoings before blaming fate.
A wise steward checks tomorrow's prophecies before today's market stroll.
Celebrate frugal victories, for discipline grows where praise is given.
Missed plans are not failures, only signals to adjust thy strategy.
Beware the merchant's siren call: not all that glitters requires thy gold.
A budget observed is a fortress defended; a budget ignored invites siege.
Thy predictions speak truth only when fed honest deeds—lie not to thine own ledger.
The difference between treasure and trinket lies in need, not in shine.
He who tracks his spending rules his fate; he who ignores it serves chaos.
A subscription forgotten is a thief in thy treasury, silent but persistent.
Better a peasant's feast paid in full than a lord's banquet bought on promises.
Compound interest: the dragon that guards thy hoard or devours it—choose thy allegiance wisely.
Methinks thy tavern visits grow too frequent. Remember: ale tastes sweeter when not bought with tomorrow's bread.
Sir Spendalot himself spent 12 gold on a crystal ball. Did it predict his regret? It did not.
Thy budget spreadsheet from yonder Google hath been vanquished. All hail the new realm!
A wise knight once said: 'I'll just check my balance quickly.' He did not. He browsed for an hour.
Fear not the red numbers, for they are but ink. Fear instead the habits that summoned them.
Prophecy reveals the future; discipline shapes it. Both require effort, alas.
The market cares not for thy noble intentions—bring gold or bring naught.
Every unconfirmed prediction is a knot in the thread of fate. Tidy thy prophecies!
Subcategories exist not to complicate, but to illuminate. Use them, and see patterns emerge.
If thou knowest not where thy money went, thou art already on the path to knowing where more will go.
The path to solvency is paved with small, boring choices made consistently.
Sir Spendalot predicts thy balance, not thy choices. Choose wisely, for the math is ruthless.
If thou art reading this tooltip instead of recording thy expense, thou art procrastinating. I know. I, too, procrastinate.
A coffee is but €3. Thirty coffees? A week's groceries. Math cares not for thy morning ritual.
Grocery shopping whilst hungry invites the overspending demons. Eat first, shop second.
The 'I deserve this' justification hath felled more budgets than any dragon.
Subscription services: the modern plague upon thy treasury. Audit them quarterly, or perish monthly.
That 'small purchase' made daily becomes a dragon's hoard... withdrawn from thy hoard.
Welcome back, steward of coin! Thy treasury awaits thy scrutiny.
Hail, keeper of the purse! Another day, another chance to guard thy gold.
Knowledge is power, but only when followed by action. What wilt thou change?
